theme: shelters sited for the worst flood-day traffic
title: Shelters Sited for the Worst Flood-Day Traffic
seed_problem: facility placement on a road network minimizing worst-case evacuation time
mutation: composite: distance-based facility placement is replaced by a capacitated-flow-evacuation objective that must be equalized across a published sweep of blockage scenarios -- the goal becomes siting shelters so that every scenario's surviving min-cut (not any single scenario, and not proximity) stays healthy, coupling facility-placement + blockage-scenario-sweep + capacitated-flow-evacuation into one non-additive max-over-scenarios objective.
strategies: trivial: take the first S candidate sites in input presentation order (reproduces the checker baseline)

naive greedy: BFS hop-distance/coverage siting on the raw unblocked road graph, always preferring the nearest possible site to each district -- ignores capacities and every published blockage scenario entirely

insight (strong): reformulate the objective via max-flow/min-cut -- completion time per scenario is governed by the surviving cut capacity to the chosen shelters, so build the shelter set by marginal-gain on the worst-scenario max-flow (computed with real per-scenario Dinic), then locally improve by swaps; this explicitly equalizes surviving cut capacity across every scenario instead of chasing proximity
